title: System and method for facilitating an objective-oriented data structure and an objective via the data structure
abstract: In some embodiments, a hierarchical tree may include nodes that each indicates an objective, and each non-root node of the nodes indicates an objective supporting an objective of at least one node with which the non-root node has a direct relationship. Based on a modification to an objective of a given node of the tree, a determination of whether at least one child node of the given node indicates an unsatisfiable objective (e.g., no longer suitably supporting the given node's objective). For a child node of the given node determined to indicate an unsatisfiable objective, a corresponding subset of the tree may be replaced with replacement nodes that each indicates a satisfiable objective (e.g., suitably supporting the given node's objective), where the corresponding subset includes (i) the child node and (ii) descendant nodes of the child node.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system for facilitating data structure modification of an objective-oriented hierarchical tree, a computer system that comprises one or more processors programmed with computer program instructions that, when executed, cause the computer system to: store tree nodes of a hierarchical tree and relationships between the tree nodes, each of the tree nodes indicating an objective, each non-root node of the tree nodes indicating an objective supporting an objective of at least one node with which the non root node has a direct relationship; detect a modification to an objective of a given node of the tree nodes; determine, based on the detected modification, child nodes of the given node and whether at least one of the child nodes of the given node indicates an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node; and for a child node of the child nodes determined to indicate an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node, cause replacement of a corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree with replacement nodes that each indicates an objective suitably supporting the objective of the given node, the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprising (i) the child node and (ii) one or more descendant nodes of the child node. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree is not based on any determination of whether grandchild nodes of the given node indicate an objective that suitably supports the objective of the given node. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the computer system is caused to: provide a collection of hierarchical trees as input to a neural network to cause the neural network to be trained to predict a candidate tree subset for a given objective such that a root node of the candidate tree subset indicates an objective that supports the given objective; and provide the modified objective to the neural network to cause the neural network to predict a given tree subset comprising the replacement nodes, 

 wherein causing the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises causing the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree to be replaced with the given tree subset predicted by the neural network. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein the given tree subset is at least a subset of a previously- generated hierarchical tree stored in one or more databases. 5. The system of claim 3, wherein the neural network outputs a prediction of the given tree subset and one or more other tree subsets based on the modified objective, wherein the computer system is caused to: select the given tree subset over the one or more other tree subsets to replace the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree based on a root node of the given tree subset indicating an objective similar to at least one reference objective. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the computer system is caused to: generate a task requesting user input related to one or more objectives to support the objective of the given node; obtain, based on the task, sets of user inputs indicating crowd-sourced objectives; and select, from the sets of user inputs, one or more crowd-sourced objectives to be one or more reference objectives, the at least one reference objective being at least one crowd-sourced objective of the one or more crowd-sourced objectives, wherein selecting the given tree subset to replace the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises selecting the given tree subset based on the objective of the root node of the given tree subset being similar to the at least one crowd-sourced objective. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the computer system is caused to: generate a task requesting user input related to one or more objectives to support the objective of the given node; obtain, based on the task, sets of user inputs indicating crowd-sourced objectives; and select, from the sets of user inputs, one or more crowd-sourced objectives to indicated in the replacement nodes, 

 wherein causing the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises causing the replacement of the corresponding subset with one or more nodes indicating the one or more crowd-sourced objectives. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises (i) the child node of the given node, (ii) the one or more descendant nodes of the child node, and (iii) other child nodes of the given node. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein causing the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises causing the replacement of the corresponding subset without regard to whether all of the other child nodes of the given node indicate an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node. 10. A method implemented by a computer system that comprises one or more processors executing computer program instructions that, when executed, perform the method, the method comprising: storing tree nodes of a hierarchical tree and relationships between the tree nodes, each of the tree nodes indicating an objective, each non-root node of the tree nodes indicating an objective supporting an objective of at least one node with which the non-root node has a direct relationship; detecting a modification to an objective of a given node of the tree nodes; determining, based on the detected modification, child nodes of the given node and whether at least one of the child nodes of the given node indicates an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node; and for a child node of the child nodes determined to indicate an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node, causing replacement of a corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree with replacement nodes that each indicates an objective suitably supporting the objective of the given node, the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprising (i) the child node and (ii) one or more descendant nodes of the child node. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree is not based on any determination of whether grandchild nodes of the given node indicate an objective that suitably supports the objective of the given node. 12. The method of claim 10, further comprising: providing a collection of hierarchical trees as input to a prediction model to cause the prediction model to be trained to predict a candidate tree subset for a given objective such that a root node of the candidate tree subset indicates an objective that supports the given objective; and providing the modified objective to the prediction model to cause the prediction model to predict a given tree subset comprising the replacement nodes, wherein causing the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises causing the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree to be replaced with the given tree subset predicted by the prediction model. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the given tree subset is at least a subset of a previously- generated hierarchical tree stored in one or more databases. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the prediction model outputs a prediction of the given tree subset and one or more other tree subsets based on the modified objective, the method further comprising: selecting the given tree subset over the one or more other tree subsets to replace the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree based on a root node of the given tree subset indicating an objective similar to at least one reference objective. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising: generating a task requesting user input related to one or more objectives to support the objective of the given node; obtaining, based on the task, sets of user inputs indicating crowd-sourced objectives; and 

 selecting, from the sets of user inputs, one or more crowd-sourced objectives to be one or more reference objectives, the at least one reference objective being at least one crowd-sourced objective of the one or more crowd-sourced objectives, wherein selecting the given tree subset to replace the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises selecting the given tree subset based on the objective of the root node of the given tree subset being similar to the at least one crowd-sourced objective. 16. The method of claim 10, further comprising: generating a task requesting user input related to one or more objectives to support the objective of the given node; obtaining, based on the task, sets of user inputs indicating crowd-sourced objectives; and selecting, from the sets of user inputs, one or more crowd-sourced objectives to indicated in the replacement nodes, wherein causing the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises causing the replacement of the corresponding subset with one or more nodes indicating the one or more crowd-sourced objectives. 17. The method of claim 10, wherein the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises (i) the child node of the given node, (ii) the one or more descendant nodes of the child node, and (iii) other child nodes of the given node. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein causing the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprises causing the replacement of the corresponding subset without regard to whether all of the other child nodes of the given node indicate an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node. 19. A system comprising: a computer system that comprises one or more processors programmed with computer program instructions that, when executed, cause the computer system to: 

 provide a collection of hierarchical trees as input to a prediction model to cause the prediction model to be trained to predict a candidate tree subset for a given objective such that a root node of the candidate tree subset indicates an objective that supports the given objective; and store tree nodes of a hierarchical tree and relationships between the tree nodes, each of the tree nodes indicating an objective, each non-root node of the tree nodes indicating an objective supporting an objective of at least one node with which the non root node has a direct relationship; detect a modification to an objective of a given node of the tree nodes; determine, based on the detected modification, child nodes of the given node and whether at least one of the child nodes of the given node indicates an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node; and provide the modified objective to the prediction model to cause the prediction model to predict a given tree subset comprising replacement nodes that each indicates an objective suitably supporting the objective of the given node; for a child node of the child nodes determined to indicate an objective that no longer suitably supports the objective of the given node, cause replacement of a corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree with the given tree subset predicted by the prediction model, the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree comprising (i) the child node and (ii)one or more descendant nodes of the child node. 20. The system of claim 19, wherein the replacement of the corresponding subset of the hierarchical tree is not based on any determination of whether grandchild nodes of the given node indicate an objective that suitably supports the objective of the given node.
